content: MediaPipe 0.10.14 Hands API for 2-hand detection: change max_num_hands=1 to max_num_hands=2 in Hands() constructor. Access handedness via results.multi_handedness[i].classification[0].label ('Left' or 'Right') and results.multi_handedness[i].classification[0].score (>= 0.5 always). Indexing: multi_handedness[i] is aligned with multi_hand_landmarks[i] — use zip(results.multi_hand_landmarks, results.multi_handedness) for safe iteration. Handedness assumes mirrored camera input; since camera.py already does cv2.flip(frame, 1), labels match the user's perspective correctly (Left label = user's left hand). Known issue: in some versions multi_handedness index may be misaligned — zip() pattern is the robust approach. Performance: model_complexity=0 (lite) with 2 hands achieves 30-60fps on desktop hardware. Rough overhead: ~20-30% more inference time vs 1 hand.
